flashcards.definition: A vertical line x = a where the function → ±∞; occurs where the denominator is zero (after cancellation).
A horizontal line y = L that the function approaches as x → ±∞.
A linear asymptote y = mx + b that occurs when the numerator's degree is exactly one more than the denominator's.
n < m → y = 0; n = m → y = aₙ/bₘ; n > m → no HA.
The function increases or decreases without bound (→ +∞ or → −∞) on either side.
